# RoamCode

> A self-hosted control plane for the real Claude Code or Codex TUI. RoamCode runs each coding-agent CLI on your machine with its existing provider login, keeps every Session alive in tmux, and lets an authorized browser operate it without translating the terminal into a chat. MIT-licensed.

Execution, source code, provider credentials, and terminal state remain on the user's machine. Remote access uses loopback, a private network, VPN, or an HTTPS reverse proxy owned by the operator. RoamCode has no hosted service dependency. macOS and Linux; Windows via WSL2.

The canonical product navigation is Sessions, Automations, and Agents. Agents is Node-first; an Agent is a concrete Claude Code, Codex, or installed adapter runtime on one Node. Every Automation Run creates a new real Session on one exact Node, runtime, and working directory.

## Docs

- [README — product overview and three-step quickstart](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode#readme)
- [Documentation index](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode/blob/main/docs/README.md)

## Install

- macOS with Homebrew: `brew install burakgon/roamcode/roamcode && roamcode install`
- macOS or Linux with Node.js 24+ and tmux: `curl -fsSL https://roamcode.ai/install | bash`
- The installer verifies service health and creates the first five-minute, one-use browser pairing link.
- [Repository](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode)
- [Discussions](https://github.com/burakgon/roamcode/discussions)
